attack_surface.data_description: Claim proof (e.g., Merkle proof) and parameters (amount, user address)

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof is forged or inaccurate, allowing attacker to claim incentives for others.

actor_risk.description: An external user could potentially use a forged proof to claim entitlements they do not own.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing leads to theft of incentive tokens from legitimate users.

assumptions: The BGT Incentive Distributor contract's proof verification is faulty or can be bypassed.
An attacker can obtain or generate a valid-looking forged proof for another user's claim.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust Merkle proof verification in the smart contract.

description: An attacker spoofs a user's claim entitlement by providing a forged proof to the BGT Incentive Distributor contract, stealing incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim transaction data (amount, recipient address, proof)

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: Claim transaction parameters (e.g., amount) are altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: An external user could potentially alter claim details to steal more or redirect funds if the transaction is not properly signed and verified.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with claim transactions can lead to theft or incorrect distribution of assets.

assumptions: The claim transaction is not signed over all critical parameters.
An attacker can intercept and modify the transaction before it reaches the network.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical transaction parameters are included in the signature.

description: An attacker tampers with the claim transaction data to alter the amount claimed or redirect incentives to their own address.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient validation allows claiming more than entitled or claiming for others.

actor_risk.description: An external user could gain unauthorized privilege to claim assets they are not entitled to or claim assets belonging to others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting validation flaws leads to unauthorized access and theft of assets.

assumptions: The BGT Incentive Distributor contract lacks sufficient checks on claim proof validity or claimed amounts.
The contract does not correctly enforce that the claimant is the rightful owner of the entitlement.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict access control and validation logic in the smart contract for claiming.

description: An attacker elevates their privilege to claim incentives they are not entitled to or claim incentives belonging to other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Queue Boost transaction details (validator, BGT amount, user address)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction is initiated without proper authentication/signature from the legitimate user.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to initiate queue boost transactions on behalf of others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spoofed transactions can lead to unauthorized manipulation of BGT boosting and validator influence.

assumptions: The transaction is not properly signed by the user's wallet.
The Governance Contract fails to verify the transaction sender against the intended user.

mitigation.strategy: Require standard EVM transaction signing for all actions and verify sender address in contract.

description: An attacker spoofs a user's identity to queue a validator boost using their BGT without authorization.

attack_surface.data_description: Queue Boost transaction data (validator, BGT amount)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters (validator, amount) are altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to alter the details of a queue boost transaction initiated by someone else.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with queue boost transactions can lead to misallocation of BGT boosts.

assumptions: The queue boost transaction is not signed over all critical parameters.
An attacker can intercept and modify the transaction before it reaches the network.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical transaction parameters are included in the signature.

description: An attacker tampers with a user's queue boost transaction to change the target validator or the amount of BGT being queued.

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient validation allows queuing boosts for others, without sufficient BGT, or bypassing limits.

actor_risk.description: An external user could gain unauthorized privilege to queue boosts for arbitrary addresses, with arbitrary amounts, or bypass contract-defined limits.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting validation flaws allows manipulation of the BGT boosting mechanism.

assumptions: The Governance Contract lacks sufficient checks on the sender's identity or BGT balance when queuing boosts.
The contract does not enforce limits on the amount of BGT that can be queued.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict access control, balance checks, and limit enforcement in the smart contract for queuing boosts.

description: An attacker elevates their privilege to queue validator boosts on behalf of other users, queue excessive amounts of BGT, or bypass other restrictions.

attack_surface.data_description: Activate Boost transaction details (user address, target validator, amount)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction is initiated without proper authentication/signature from the legitimate user.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to initiate activate boost transactions on behalf of others.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spoofed activation leads to unauthorized manipulation of BGT boosting, potentially at an inopportune time for the user.

assumptions: The transaction is not properly signed by the user's wallet.
The Governance Contract fails to verify the transaction sender against the intended user.

mitigation.strategy: Require standard EVM transaction signing for all actions and verify sender address in contract.

description: An attacker spoofs a user's identity to activate their queued validator boost without authorization.

attack_surface.data_description: Activate Boost transaction data (user address, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters (target validator) are altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: An external user could attempt to alter the details of an activate boost transaction initiated by someone else.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with activate boost transactions can lead to misallocation of BGT boosts.

assumptions: The activate boost transaction is not signed over all critical parameters.
An attacker can intercept and modify the transaction before it reaches the network.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all critical transaction parameters are included in the signature.

description: An attacker tampers with a user's activate boost transaction to change the target validator.

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient validation allows activating boosts for others or bypassing the queue time.

actor_risk.description: An external user could gain unauthorized privilege to activate queued boosts belonging to other users or activate boosts before the required queue time has elapsed.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting validation flaws allows manipulation of the BGT boosting process, affecting validator selection and user rewards.

assumptions: The Governance Contract lacks sufficient checks on the sender's identity when activating boosts.
The contract does not correctly enforce the queue time requirement before allowing activation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict access control and state checks (queue time elapsed) in the smart contract for activating boosts.

description: An attacker elevates their privilege to activate queued validator boosts on behalf of other users or bypass the mandatory queueing period.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Berachain Hub to BGT Incentive Distributor

security_gap.gap_description: The smart contract is susceptible to resource exhaustion from excessive malicious requests.

actor_risk.description: An external user can send excessive transactions to the contract, potentially consuming all available gas or exploiting expensive operations.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A DoS attack can prevent legitimate users from claiming their earned incentives.

assumptions: The BGT Incentive Distributor contract's claim function is computationally expensive or vulnerable to spam.
The blockchain network cannot handle a large volume of malicious transactions targeted at this contract.

mitigation.strategy: Optimize contract functions for gas efficiency; Implement state rent or other layer 1 anti-DoS measures.

description: An attacker floods the BGT Incentive Distributor contract with transactions, causing denial of service and preventing users from claiming incentives.

security_gap.gap_description: The smart contract is susceptible to resource exhaustion from excessive malicious requests.

actor_risk.description: An external user can send excessive transactions to the contract, potentially consuming all available gas or exploiting expensive operations.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A DoS attack can prevent legitimate users from queueing or activating boosts, disrupting the boosting mechanism.

assumptions: The Governance Contract's queue/activate functions are computationally expensive or vulnerable to spam.
The blockchain network cannot handle a large volume of malicious transactions targeted at this contract.

mitigation.strategy: Optimize contract functions for gas efficiency; Implement state rent or other layer 1 anti-DoS measures.

description: An attacker floods the Governance Contract with queue/activate boost transactions, causing denial of service and preventing users from managing their boosts.
